summary: Iteration 77 verified: EsServicePage now consumes the shared relatedRoutes.js registry via resolvePageIdFromUrl(url). All 10 target Spanish bespoke pages (Nice, Cannes, Monaco, Milan not tested individually but same code path, Gare de Lyon, Gare du Nord, Gare Saint-Lazare, Gare Montparnasse, Gare Austerlitz) now render BOTH sections: the existing curated 'Tambien te puede interesar' (4 links, data-testid=es-related-*) AND the NEW registry-driven 'Otros traslados populares' (7 links, data-testid=related-route-<pageId>). All new-block hrefs point to /es/... URLs and use hrefLang='es'. Backward compat OK: EsSillaInfantil (URL not in registry) renders ONLY the curated block — no registry section. Regressions PASS: ES CDG still shows the registry block (7 ES links) via CityTransferPage path (no duplication), and RU CDG still shows 7 Russian links with hreflang=ru.

ui_bugs.issue: Literal text '\u00bfListo para reservar tu traslado privado en Paris?' rendered visibly in the mid-page CTA — the '\u00bf' escape appears as raw 7-char text instead of '¿'. Because it is JSX text (not a JS string literal), the unicode escape is not interpreted. Should be replaced with the actual '¿' character.
ui_bugs.priority: MEDIUM (visual polish, not in scope of this iteration but affects all EsServicePage-rendered ES pages)

test_results.test_1_es_nice: PASS - /es/traslado-aeropuerto-niza renders BOTH: 4 curated es-related-* links (Cannes, Monaco, conductor-privado, minivan) + 7 registry related-route-* links (cannes, monaco, milan, roma, barcelona, cdg, paris-airport), all /es/... hrefs, all hreflang=es. Both section titles present.
test_results.test_2_es_gare_de_lyon: PASS - /es/traslado-estacion-gare-de-lyon-paris renders 4 curated + 7 registry links (all 4 other gares + paris-train + cdg + disneyland), all /es/... hrefs, hreflang=es.
test_results.test_3_es_cannes: PASS - 4 curated + 7 registry (nice, monaco, milan, roma, barcelona, cdg, paris-airport).
test_results.test_4_es_monaco: PASS - 4 curated + 7 registry (nice, cannes, milan, roma, barcelona, cdg, paris-airport).
test_results.test_5_es_gare_du_nord: PASS - 4 curated + 7 registry (gare-de-lyon, gare-montparnasse, gare-saint-lazare, gare-austerlitz, paris-train, cdg, orly).
test_results.test_9_regression_es_cdg: PASS - /es/traslado-aeropuerto-charles-de-gaulle (uses shared CityTransferPage, not EsServicePage) still shows 7 /es/... registry links, no curated block, no duplication.
test_results.test_10_regression_ru_cdg: PASS - /taksi-iz-aeroporta-cdg still shows 7 Russian links with hreflang=ru and Cyrillic-slug hrefs (/taksi-iz-aeroporta-orli, /transfer-disneyland-parizh, etc.). No ES leakage.
test_results.test_11_backward_compat_es_silla_infanti: PASS - /es/traslado-aeropuerto-paris-silla-infantil (URL not in CITY_ROUTES registry) shows ONLY the curated 4-link 'Tambien te puede interesar' block. No registry block rendered — resolvePageIdFromUrl returned null and the IIFE early-returned. Backward compat preserved.

action_items: OPTIONAL POLISH: Fix the literal '\u00bf' text on EsServicePage.js line 148 — replace with the actual '¿' character so mid-page CTA reads '¿Listo para reservar tu traslado privado en Paris?' on all EsServicePage-driven pages.
critical_code_review_comments: resolvePageIdFromUrl() correctly reverse-maps by iterating all localized URLs. O(languages * routes) — trivially fast for ~23 routes × 5 langs = 115 comparisons. Fine.

The registry-driven block on EsServicePage is guarded by early-return when pageId is not found, so pages like EsSillaInfantil, EsConductorPrivado, EsMinivan, EsHoteles (URLs not in CITY_ROUTES) safely render only the curated block — good backward compat.

Duplication observation carried from iteration 76: EsServicePage now renders both a curated block AND a registry block on the same page. Curated block has 4 hand-picked links, registry block has 7 canonical links. There is functional overlap (e.g., ES Nice curated includes Cannes+Monaco which are also in registry). If SEO team wants deduplication, consider filtering curated links out of the registry array before render. Non-blocking; both blocks serve slightly different purposes (curated may include non-city pages like conductor-privado, registry is city-to-city cross-link).
Pre-existing bug outside iteration scope: literal '\u00bf' rendered on line 148. JSX text does not interpret \u escapes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: EsServicePage now renders TWO related sections (curated + registry-driven) when its `url` prop matches a CITY_ROUTES entry. To verify future ES bespoke pages: check `[data-testid^="related-route-"]` for 6-8 elements, ALL hrefs starting with /es/, hreflang=es. Curated block uses `[data-testid^="es-related-"]`. If a new ES bespoke page needs the registry block, ensure its `url` prop matches a CITY_ROUTES `urls.es` value exactly (or add the URL to relatedRoutes.js).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — iteration 77 correctly closes the gap identified in iteration_76 by wiring EsServicePage to the shared registry via resolvePageIdFromUrl(url). All previously-failing bespoke ES pages now render the same-language internal-linking block.
